Recent ground hail reports (NoVA)

How heat is computed. Hail heat blends recent radar swath activity weighted by your recency tiers (last 6 months ×2.0, 6–12 months ×1.5; counts per ~11×14 mi MRMS grid cell) with long-term size severity (≥1" and ≥2" swath counts since Mar 2015, 2"+ weighted ×3). Cells are smoothed to ZIP centroids (inverse-distance). Combined target = 60% aluminum-home density (Fairfax assessor registry) + 40% hail heat. WALL-TYPE RULE (July 20, 2026): pockets and live lookups include all assessor codes that are ≥50% aluminum exterior (Aluminum Siding, Aluminum w/ Brick Trim, Half Aluminum/Half Brick, half-stone, half-block); each pocket home carries its exact assessor code in the CSV material column. Pockets are full-ZIP county-wide joins (no tax-map zone shortcut). SINGLE-FAMILY RULE (June 11, 2026): house lists and D2D routes are single-family detached ONLY — the embedded pocket is parcel-verified, and live full-ZIP lookups run the 3-table join (dwelling wall type × parcels land use × address points), excluding townhomes/duplexes/attached. Optional metal-roof inclusion (modal checkbox) adds dwellings whose roof material matches aluminum/metal/steel/tin codes, under the same single-family filter; the CSV then carries a material column with the assessor's exact roof description. The county-level and per-ZIP aluminum ESTIMATES on the map/table still include townhomes until the registry re-count — treat those numbers as upper bounds. Snapshot baked June 10, 2026; when live PLRB queries succeed, recent activity refreshes and the min-size filter activates with full per-event size weighting (0.5–0.75 ×0.25 · 0.75–1 ×0.5 · 1–1.5 ×1 · 1.5–2 ×1.75 · 2+ ×3).
